{"query":"Tesla Cybercab official retail purchase price target Elon Musk announcement","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1761,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.foxbusiness.com/fox-news-tech/elon-musk-reveals-price-teslas-cybercab","title":"Tesla CEO Elon Musk confirms Cybercab will sell for $30,000 by 2027 | Fox Business","content":""Elon – to be clear – the bet was that Tesla wouldn't sell a Cybercab to a customer for $30k or less before 2027," the user asked.
The "bet" referenced in the exchange dates back to 2024, when Musk first unveiled the long-anticipated robotaxi and said it would cost less than $30,000 and enter production in 2026.
YouTuber Marques Brownlee, known as MKBHD, publicly questioned at the time whether Tesla could hit that target by 2027, saying he would shave his head on camera if Musk proved him wrong."},{"url":"https://finance.yahoo.com/news/elon-musk-warns-teslas-25k-170112643.html","title":"Elon Musk Warns Tesla's $25K Cybercab Will Be 'Agonizingly Slow' At First — Even As He Targets 2M Robotaxis A Year","content":"February 12, 2026 2 min read
Tesla is set to begin producing the Cybercab robotaxi in April, as it also works toward bringing its Optimus humanoid robot into production.
The two-seat Cybercab is designed without a steering wheel or pedals, is expected to cost about $25,000, and is part of Musk's goal of reaching long-term production of 2 million robotaxis a year.
Optimus is designed for unsafe, repetitive or boring tasks, and Tesla has said it is planning for an eventual capacity of 1 million robots per year."},{"url":"https://www.basenor.com/blogs/news/tesla-cybercab-retail-sales-confirmed-for-under-30k-in-2026","title":"Tesla Cybercab: Retail Sales Confirmed for Under $30k in 2026","content":"The News: Elon Musk has confirmed Tesla will sell the Cybercab directly to consumers for under $30,000 in 2026, not exclusively for its internal robotaxi fleet.
While Musk's confirmation is direct, the actual retail availability hinges on regulatory approval for unsupervised Full Self-Driving operation.
What makes this announcement particularly intriguing is the ownership model it enables.
According to previous Tesla communications, Cybercab owners will be able to add their vehicles to Tesla's robotaxi network when not in personal use, potentially generating revenue to offset the purchase price.
The BASENOR Editorial Desk covers Tesla, SpaceX, and related technology, curating reporting from primary sources — official accounts, regulatory filings, and software release data.
Read our editorial standards or email editorial@basenor.com to report an error."},{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/02/tesla-tsla-q2-2026-vehicle-delivery-production.html","title":"Tesla (TSLA) Q2 2026 vehicle delivery production","content":"A Tesla-compiled consensus said analysts were expecting 406,024 deliveries, while StreetAccount's average estimate was 406,600.
European car buyers purchased more Tesla and other EVs in the first half of the year.
Musk has directed Tesla to focus on ramping production and sales of its Semi electric trucks, and to start production of its driverless Cybercab.
In its Energy business, which installs solar photovoltaics and sells battery energy storage systems, Tesla said it deployed 13.5 GWh in the second quarter of 2026, compared to 9.6 Gwh a year ago.
Musk's SpaceX, which owns xAI, bought $269 million worth of Tesla Megapacks in April, according to its IPO filing.
Last year, SpaceX spent $131 million purchasing Tesla Cybertrucks."}]}

He shared the update in a post","score":0.72395444,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.basenor.com/blogs/news/tesla-cybercab-starting-price-confirmed-at-25-000","title":"Tesla Cybercab Starting Price Confirmed at $25,000","content":"Free Shipping in the USA - Shop now. # Tesla Cybercab Starting Price Confirmed at $25,000. Tesla's Cybercab has a confirmed starting price: $25,000. That number, circulating widely after a post from Whole Mars Catalog, aligns with what Elon Musk stated during Tesla's Q3 2024 earnings call — refining an earlier 'under $30,000' figure first floated at the vehicle's October 2024 unveiling. For a fully autonomous, two-passenger EV with no steering wheel or pedals, it's a number that will get a lot of people paying attention. Whole Mars Catalog tweet confirming Tesla Cybercab starting price of $25,000. To put the $25,000 figure in context: Musk has described it as roughly the cost to produce the vehicle, and the price point it will occupy as a car without traditional driver controls. At $25,000, Tesla is positioning it well below the cost of a new Model 3, which makes the math interesting both for individual buyers and for fleet operators building out autonomous ride-hailing networks.","score":0.71995544,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.87,"request_id":"647bea94-b288-4816-a936-8115b77c1446"}
